definition: China's fiscal pivot from property-sector-financed infrastructure to central government bond-financed strategic industrial investment shifts the commodity demand mix: copper intensity per unit of GDP rising for the first time since 2012, iron ore intensity declining.
evidence: Special purpose bond allocation data showing structural shift
Grid investment: 8-12kt copper per GW installed capacity
EV production: 80-100kg copper per vehicle vs. 20-25kg ICE
Property-related steel demand down 15-20% from 2021 peaks
Chinese copper demand growing 4-6% annually; steel demand declining 2-3%
open_questions: Will policy revert toward property stimulus, re-weighting toward iron ore?
How quickly does the demand mix shift propagate to global commodity balances?
Do historical GDP-to-commodity-demand elasticities need wholesale revision?
